title: Home agent session: loading skeleton while history loads
prompt:
On opening the page, the Kanban home/sidebar agent session shows a blank gap for a moment, then messages pop in with no transition. Add a proper loading animation (skeleton / spinner) for the 'initial history loading' state.

Root cause (already traced):
- web-ui/src/hooks/use-kanban-chat-session.ts computes an internal `isLoading` for the initial `onLoadMessages` fetch, but does NOT expose it distinctly — it folds it into `isSending` (`isSending: isSending || isLoading`). So consumers can't tell 'loading history' from 'sending a turn' or 'idle empty'.
- web-ui/src/components/home-agent/home-sidebar-agent-panel.tsx only renders a <Spinner> (line ~149) while `runtimeProjectConfig` is missing; during chat-history load the message area is just empty → blank-then-pop.

What to do:
- Expose the initial-load state from useKanbanChatSession (e.g. return `isLoading` distinctly, keeping current behavior intact / not breaking the isSending consumers).
- In the chat panel / KanbanAgentChatPanel message area, when loading history AND no messages yet, render a loading affordance (skeleton message rows preferred, or a centered spinner) instead of empty space.
- Don't show the history-loading skeleton during normal send/stream (that already has its own indicator).
- Follow web-ui stack rules in AGENTS.md (Spinner from @/components/ui/spinner, Tailwind surface tokens, no console.*). Keep it subtle/dark-theme.

Acceptance:
- Opening a home session shows a skeleton/spinner immediately, replaced by messages when loaded.
- No regression to send/cancel indicators or empty-idle state.
- Existing useKanbanChatSession tests updated; web:typecheck + biome clean.
